en: You know, rather than be scared by a jobless future,
I started to rethink it and I said,
I could really be excited by a jobless future
ko: 일자리 없는 미래에 오히려 신이 날 수도 있겠다고

en: because I can finally see a day in under 50 years
ko: 왜냐하면 50년 안에 드디어 그런 날이 올 거니까요

en: when people don't have to work for pay
ko: 사람들이 돈을 위해 일하지 않아도 되는 날이요

en: because humans have labored for thousands of years,
slaves to their work primarily in most of the world
and soon, they'll be able to do what they wanted to do,
ko: 그리고 곧, 그들이 하고 싶었던 일을 할 수 있게 될 거예요,

en: what they were born to do before someone came along
ko: 태어날 때부터 하고 싶었던 일을, 누군가가 와서

en: and said, stop doing that, you gotta go earn a living.
ko: 그만하고 생계를 벌어라, 라고 말하기 전에 하던 일을요.

en: This is a whole new renaissance
that we may be able to see actually in our lifetime
ko: 우리 생전에 실제로 볼 수도 있는

en: where humans can do what they were born to do
ko: 인간이 태어날 때부터 하도록 된 일을 할 수 있는 시대요

en: like art and dance and music and invention
ko: 예술, 춤, 음악, 발명 같은 것들이요

en: and caring for others and whatever it is
that you wanna do.
Now, there'll be fits and starts,
there'll be politics that get in the way,
this is no utopia, no, it is a utopia actually,
ko: 이건 유토피아가 아니야, 아니, 사실 유토피아가 맞아,

en: come to think of it,
and working together towards this goal,
ko: 그리고 이 목표를 향해 함께 노력하면,

en: this is actually a great idea
when we look at it from this vantage point.
